parameters.jsonOutput: { "data": { "tier": "tier_1_image", "auto_approve": false, "brand_tone": "warm_reassuring", "pain_points": ["fear of pain", "cost anxiety", "dental phobia", "time constraints"], "ai_generate_images": true } }

nodes.name: Get Preferences
nodes.notes: MOCK of GET /v1/content/preferences. Replace with HTTP Request when backend is live. ai_generate_images decides AI-generate vs library-match branch (library branch added at go-live).

nodes.name: Get Business
nodes.notes: MOCK of GET /v1/settings/business. Replace with HTTP Request when backend is live.

nodes.name: Generate Image
nodes.notes: Calls OpenAI images endpoint directly via HTTP (avoids the n8n image node's stale response_format/style params). Model gpt-image-1 returns data[0].b64_json (base64, no url). Body sent as key-value fields so n8n serializes the long prompt safely. Send only model/prompt/size — gpt-image-1 rejects response_format & style. Costs ~$0.04-0.08 per render. At go-live: map size per platform (gpt-image-1 sizes: 1024x1024, 1536x1024, 1024x1536, auto) and add the media-library (tag-match) branch when ai_generate_images=false.

nodes.name: Image to File
nodes.notes: Converts gpt-image-1 base64 (data[0].b64_json) into a viewable image binary so n8n shows a preview. At go-live this binary is uploaded via POST /v1/media/assets.
